weather_report:
Late morning in Subiaco opens clean and bright, the kind of blue that sharpens edges and makes every leaf look freshly cut. The sun has an eager bite as it climbs, and even in late April the UV can nip at bare skin, so a hat and sunscreen earn their keep. Air clarity is good—crisp sightlines, little haze—so the day feels honest and tidy, the sort that invites unhurried plans without asking much in return.

Expect the shine to hold through lunchtime before a brush of scattered clouds softens the glare in the afternoon, more glossy than gloomy. It looks pleasantly mild rather than sweltering. Dress in light layers—short sleeves with a throw-on for shade or a breeze—plus sunglasses for the hard white light. Good hours for small rituals: a stroll to a café veranda, a leisurely loop through Kings Park’s paths, tending balcony herbs, or running errands by bike while the streets are calm. If you’ll be out through midday, chase the patches of shade and reapply sunscreen; the sun will feel friendly, but it is not shy.
